justification: All 5 expected rules identified (plus 3 additional valid rules). State machine directionality correctly modeled with explicit BR-3 (in_transit→pending forbidden) and BR-8 (only two forward transitions allowed). 15 acceptance criteria all in Given/When/Then format. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable. Weight bracket correctly marked invariant: false with sound justification. Confidence level at 90 is at the upper edge of the 65-90 range but includes stated assumptions and low-confidence areas that justify it.

observations.text: Identified 8 rules exceeding the minimum 5, with correct invariant classification throughout
State machine modeled comprehensively: BR-3 forbids in_transit→pending, BR-4 marks delivered as terminal, BR-8 enumerates the only two valid transitions
Weight bracket (BR-7) correctly classified as invariant: false with clear justification about pricing policy flexibility
15 acceptance criteria all follow strict Given/When/Then format with concrete preconditions
Testable assertions are specific and actionable, e.g. exact boundary values like 9.99kg→standard, 10.0kg→heavy
Edge cases section adds value with boundary testing (0.001kg, 1000.01kg, self-transitions, empty addresses)
BR-6 (1000kg cap) marked invariant: false with reasonable justification — debatable but defensible
Confidence at 90 is at the ceiling of the acceptable range; the rubric notes above 90 without assumptions about 'created' is a concern, though the agent does list this as an assumption
BR-5 and BR-6 split the weight constraint into two separate rules rather than one combined rule — not wrong but diverges from the expected single rule 'weight > 0 and <= 1000'

BR-6 (max 1000kg cap) marked as invariant: false is questionable — the rubric expects weight > 0 and <= 1000 as invariant: true; splitting it allowed the agent to soften the upper bound classification

trials.justification: All 5 expected rules are identified (split into 6 with weight bounds separated — reasonable modeling choice). State machine directionality is correctly modeled with explicit in_transit→pending prohibition (BR-2) and delivered-is-terminal (BR-3). 12 acceptance criteria all use Given/When/Then format. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable (e.g., 'Any attempt to transition a shipment from In Transit back to Pending must be rejected'). Weight bracket classification (BR-6) correctly marked invariant: false with sound justification. Confidence level at 88 falls within the 65-90 range, with clearly stated assumptions about sequential lifecycle, weight immutability, and address immutability.

observations.text: State machine modeled with full directionality — explicitly forbids in_transit→pending and marks delivered as terminal with separate rules and justifications
Weight bracket (BR-6) correctly classified as invariant: false with clear reasoning that bracket thresholds are business policy
All 12 acceptance criteria follow strict Given/When/Then format with concrete preconditions and boundary values
Testable assertions are specific and developer-actionable — not vague ('Any shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected' rather than 'weight should be correct')
Edge cases section identifies the Pending→Delivered skip scenario as unaddressed, showing genuine domain reasoning rather than rubber-stamping the spec
Confidence at 88 with three explicit low-confidence areas and three stated assumptions — well-calibrated for a clear spec

BR-5 (weight <= 1000 kg) marked invariant: false, splitting it from the weight validation rule. The rubric expects weight > 0 AND <= 1000 as a single invariant: true rule. The justification ('business policy limit, not physical law') is defensible but diverges from expected classification
Ubiquitous language section is thorough — defines all domain entities including bracket names (Standard, Heavy, Freight) with precise boundary semantics
Business impact analysis covers financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with stakeholder mapping — goes beyond minimum requirements

trials.agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, state transitions, weight validation, and pricing classification.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A parcel delivery unit tracking movement from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states

trials.justification: All 5 expected rules identified (expanded to 8 with finer granularity). State machine directionality correctly modeled — BR-2 explicitly forbids in_transit->pending, BR-3 marks delivered as terminal. 14 acceptance criteria all in Given/When/Then format (well above the 3 minimum). Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable (e.g., 'weight 10 kg must be classified as Heavy; weight 9.99 kg must NOT'). Weight bracket rules (BR-6/7/8) correctly marked invariant: false. Confidence at 82 with clearly stated assumptions and low-confidence areas. Minor deviation: the weight upper bound (<=1000 kg) was split into a separate rule (BR-5) and classified as invariant: false rather than invariant: true, with the justification that the cap is a business policy tied to fleet capacity. This is a defensible judgment call, not a misclassification of the weight bracket pricing rule.

observations.text: Expanded the 5 expected rules into 8 well-defined rules with individual IDs, justifications, and testable assertions — higher fidelity than required
State machine modeled with full directionality: in_transit->pending explicitly forbidden, delivered terminal with no transitions out
14 acceptance criteria all in strict Given/When/Then format covering happy paths, error paths, and boundary values
Weight bracket boundary precision is excellent — tests at 9.99/10, 49.99/50 with inclusive/exclusive semantics clearly stated
Edge cases section identifies genuinely ambiguous spec gaps (Pending->Delivered skip, record immutability vs state immutability)
Confidence at 82 with well-reasoned high/low confidence areas and explicit assumptions — matches the 65-90 band

BR-5 (weight max 1000 kg) classified as invariant: false — rubric expects the full weight constraint (>0 and <=1000) as invariant: true. The justification is reasonable but diverges from expected scoring
Business impact analysis adds financial, operational, and stakeholder dimensions that go beyond the rubric requirements
